steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize the combined deadweight tonnage of the Bahamas' merchant ships in 2021. The word 'combined' is the key signal — the answer must make the aggregate tonnage figure (74.3 million tons) the main point, not ship counts or definitions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The carrying capacity of a merchant ship is also known as deadweight tonnage.’: Defines a term but presents no tonnage figure and says nothing about the Bahamas' fleet. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘In 2021, the Bahamas had 1,323 merchant ships hired to carry cargo or passengers.’: Emphasizes ship count, not tonnage. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Combined, the Bahamas’ merchant ships had a deadweight tonnage of 74.3 million tons in 2021.’: Opens with 'Combined,' makes 74.3 million tons the focal point, and directly satisfies the goal. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘There were a total of 1,323 merchant ships—ships hired to carry cargo or passengers—registered in the Bahamas in 2021.’: Again emphasizes ship count; tonnage is omitted entirely. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
